name: Corn & Onion Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1 small Red onion (about 3 oz.) - thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sprinkle yeast over water; let stand about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in salt, 1 tablespoon olive oil, cornmeal, and 1 cup flour. Beat with a mixer until dough is stretchy, 3-5 minutes.
3. Add another cup of flour. Coat a board with the remaining flour. Knead dough on the board until smooth and elastic. Add more flour if the dough sticks.
4. Place dough in a bowl, cover, let rise in a warm spot, about 45 minutes.
5. Punch dough down. Pour remaining oil into a 10-inch x 15-inch baking pan.
6. Turn dough into oil to coat, then press and pat evenly to fill the pan.
7. Sprinkle with corn and sage; press firmly into dough.
8. Separate onion slices into rings and mix with lemon juice. Scatter onion rings over dough and press down gently.
9. Cover pan and let dough rise, about 30 minutes.
10. Bake uncovered on the lowest rack in a 400-degree oven until dough is well browned on edges and bottom, about 35 minutes.
11. Sprinkle with cheese and bake for 2 more minutes.
12. Serve hot, warm, or at room temperature. Cut into squares.
Serves 12. Per serving: calories 161 (22% from fat), fat 4 g, cholesterol 5 mg, sodium 120 mg.
